2026-04-20T05:56:41.8947844Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8948410Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8948821Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8949173Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8950062Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8950311Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8950468Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8950645Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8950814Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8951042Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8951245Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8951532Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T05:56:41.8951784Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. 2026-04-20T05:59:41.5845466Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T05:57:49.721673386Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58352,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Docs: Document ArmScopeInfo and scope handling for extension non-resource methods\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` (Azure Management SDK Generator)\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (#58291) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58291 unified scope information into a single `ArmScopeInfo` record type (renamed from a prior type) and added a `Scope: ArmScopeInfo` parameter to `NonResourceMethod`. Extension non-resource operations now carry full scope info (kind, ID pattern, resource type) through the generation pipeline, enabling correct scope parameter injection in generated mockable extension methods.\\n\\nKey files changed:\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/ResourceSchemas/ArmScopeInfo.cs` — renamed and updated unified scope record\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/ResourceSchemas/NonResourceMethod.cs` — now carries `ArmScopeInfo Scope`\\n- `MockableArmClientProvider.cs` and `MockableResourceProvider.cs` — use scope info to emit the scope parameter\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `docs/OperationContext.md` documents extension resources briefly (§ \\u0026#34;Extension Resources\\u0026#34;) but does not mention `ArmScopeInfo` at all, nor does it explain how scope is resolved for **non-resource** methods on extension clients\\n- Neither `docs/OperationContext.md` nor `docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md` documents the `NonResourceMethod` model or how its `Scope` field drives generated method signatures\\n- There is no documentation of the `ArmScopeInfo` record itself: its three fields (`Kind`, `ScopeIdPattern`, `ScopeResourceType`), when `ScopeResourceType` is null, and how it differs from the per-resource `ResourceScope` enum\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — extend the existing \\u0026#34;Extension Resources\\u0026#34; section and add a new \\u0026#34;Non-Resource Methods\\u0026#34; subsection after \\u0026#34;Singleton Resources\\u0026#34;:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### ArmScopeInfo\\n\\n`ArmScopeInfo` is a unified record that describes the ARM scope of any operation:\\n\\n| Field | Type | Description |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `Kind` | `ResourceScope` | Scope kind: `Tenant`, `Subscription`, `ResourceGroup`, `ManagementGroup`, or `Extension` |\\n| `ScopeIdPattern` | `RequestPathPattern` | The parsed request path that identifies the scope (e.g., `/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}`) |\\n| `ScopeResourceType` | `string?` | The ARM resource type of the scope (e.g., `\\u0026#34;Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines\\u0026#34;`). **Null** when the scope is tenant-level (empty path) or when the path contains variable resource type segments |\\n\\n`ArmScopeInfo` replaces the previous per-resource scope representations with a single, consistent type shared by both resource and non-resource operations.\\n\\n### Non-Resource Methods\\n\\nSome Azure operations are **not** associated with a specific ARM resource type. They are grouped under the `NonResourceMethod` model:\\n\\n```\\npublic record NonResourceMethod(\\n    ArmScopeInfo Scope,\\n    InputServiceMethod InputMethod,\\n    InputClient InputClient)\\n```\\n\\nThe `Scope` field carries the scope at which the operation is valid. For example, a subscription-scoped non-resource list operation will have `Scope.Kind == ResourceScope.Subscription`.\\n\\n#### How Scope Drives Method Signatures for Non-Resource Extension Methods\\n\\nWhen a non-resource method\\u0026#39;s scope kind is `Extension` (i.e., it can be applied to any ARM resource), the generator:\\n\\n1. Reads `Scope.ScopeIdPattern` to determine whether a `scope` parameter (`ResourceIdentifier`) is needed.\\n2. If the pattern is variable (not fixed to a specific resource type), the generated method in `MockableArmClientProvider` receives an explicit `ResourceIdentifier scope` parameter.\\n3. If `Scope.ScopeResourceType` is non-null, the generator can infer the scope type, and the parameter may be omitted or defaulted.\\n\\n**Example — generated method signature for an extension non-resource list:**\\n\\n```\\n// Scope is Extension with variable ScopeIdPattern → explicit scope parameter\\npublic virtual AsyncPageable GetFoosAsync(\\n    ResourceIdentifier scope,\\n    CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\nCompare to a resource-group-scoped operation where scope is contextual and the parameter is elided:\\n\\n```\\n// Scope is ResourceGroup → scope derived from Id\\npublic virtual AsyncPageable GetFoosAsync(\\n    CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — add an \\u0026#34;ArmScopeInfo and NonResourceMethod\\u0026#34; subsection to the \\u0026#34;Data Structure Mapping\\u0026#34; section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### ArmScopeInfo (unified scope type)\\n\\n`ArmScopeInfo` is produced by the emitter for both resource scopes and non-resource method scopes. It is serialised into `tspCodeModel.json` and deserialised by the C# generator:\\n\\n```\\ninterface ArmScopeInfo {\\n  kind: ResourceScope;          // e.g. \\u0026#34;Extension\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;ResourceGroup\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;Subscription\\u0026#34;\\n  scopeIdPattern: string;       // parsed request path for the scope\\n  scopeResourceType?: string;   // ARM resource type, null for tenant or variable types\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### NonResourceMethod (scope-aware)\\n\\nNon-resource methods are serialised with a `scope` field that contains the `ArmScopeInfo` for the operation:\\n\\n```\\ninterface NonResourceMethod {\\n  methodId: string;\\n  scope: ArmScopeInfo;\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe scope field is **never null** — every non-resource method must have a resolvable scope before it is included in the schema.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo code snippets are needed for these documentation-only changes.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe files being updated are generator-internal developer docs under `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/`, not a customer-facing SDK README. No standard README section check applies.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/` — ensures no C# compilation regressions\\n2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/test/Azure.Generator.Mgmt.Tests.csproj` — runs unit tests (168 tests expected)\\n3. No snippet or API export scripts are needed for documentation-only changes in `docs/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24650632981/agentic_workflow) · ● 402.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58352\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T05:55:07Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T05:55:07Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Extension non-resource operations now carry full scope info (kind, ID pattern, resource type) through the generation pipeline, enabling correct scope parameter injection in generated mockable extension methods.\\n\\nKey files changed:\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/ResourceSchemas/ArmScopeInfo.cs` — renamed and updated unified scope record\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Models/ResourceSchemas/NonResourceMethod.cs` — now carries `ArmScopeInfo Scope`\\n- `MockableArmClientProvider.cs` and `MockableResourceProvider.cs` — use scope info to emit the scope parameter\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `docs/OperationContext.md` documents extension resources briefly (§ \\u0026#34;Extension Resources\\u0026#34;) but does not mention `ArmScopeInfo` at all, nor does it explain how scope is resolved for **non-resource** methods on extension clients\\n- Neither `docs/OperationContext.md` nor `docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md` documents the `NonResourceMethod` model or how its `Scope` field drives generated method signatures\\n- There is no documentation of the `ArmScopeInfo` record itself: its three fields (`Kind`, `ScopeIdPattern`, `ScopeResourceType`), when `ScopeResourceType` is null, and how it differs from the per-resource `ResourceScope` enum\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — extend the existing \\u0026#34;Extension Resources\\u0026#34; section and add a new \\u0026#34;Non-Resource Methods\\u0026#34; subsection after \\u0026#34;Singleton Resources\\u0026#34;:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### ArmScopeInfo\\n\\n`ArmScopeInfo` is a unified record that describes the ARM scope of any operation:\\n\\n| Field | Type | Description |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `Kind` | `ResourceScope` | Scope kind: `Tenant`, `Subscription`, `ResourceGroup`, `ManagementGroup`, or `Extension` |\\n| `ScopeIdPattern` | `RequestPathPattern` | The parsed request path that identifies the scope (e.g., `/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}`) |\\n| `ScopeResourceType` | `string?` | The ARM resource type of the scope (e.g., `\\u0026#34;Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines\\u0026#34;`). **Null** when the scope is tenant-level (empty path) or when the path contains variable resource type segments |\\n\\n`ArmScopeInfo` replaces the previous per-resource scope representations with a single, consistent type shared by both resource and non-resource operations.\\n\\n### Non-Resource Methods\\n\\nSome Azure operations are **not** associated with a specific ARM resource type. They are grouped under the `NonResourceMethod` model:\\n\\n```\\npublic record NonResourceMethod(\\n    ArmScopeInfo Scope,\\n    InputServiceMethod InputMethod,\\n    InputClient InputClient)\\n```\\n\\nThe `Scope` field carries the scope at which the operation is valid. For example, a subscription-scoped non-resource list operation will have `Scope.Kind == ResourceScope.Subscription`.\\n\\n#### How Scope Drives Method Signatures for Non-Resource Extension Methods\\n\\nWhen a non-resource method\\u0026#39;s scope kind is `Extension` (i.e., it can be applied to any ARM resource), the generator:\\n\\n1. Reads `Scope.ScopeIdPattern` to determine whether a `scope` parameter (`ResourceIdentifier`) is needed.\\n2. If the pattern is variable (not fixed to a specific resource type), the generated method in `MockableArmClientProvider` receives an explicit `ResourceIdentifier scope` parameter.\\n3. If `Scope.ScopeResourceType` is non-null, the generator can infer the scope type, and the parameter may be omitted or defaulted.\\n\\n**Example — generated method signature for an extension non-resource list:**\\n\\n```\\n// Scope is Extension with variable ScopeIdPattern → explicit scope parameter\\npublic virtual AsyncPageable GetFoosAsync(\\n    ResourceIdentifier scope,\\n    CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n\\nCompare to a resource-group-scoped operation where scope is contextual and the parameter is elided:\\n\\n```\\n// Scope is ResourceGroup → scope derived from Id\\npublic virtual AsyncPageable GetFoosAsync(\\n    CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/resolve-arm-resources-integration.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — add an \\u0026#34;ArmScopeInfo and NonResourceMethod\\u0026#34; subsection to the \\u0026#34;Data Structure Mapping\\u0026#34; section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### ArmScopeInfo (unified scope type)\\n\\n`ArmScopeInfo` is produced by the emitter for both resource scopes and non-resource method scopes. 2026-04-20T05:59:41.6011485Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T05:58:14.778195209Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4288781055,\"number\":58330,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Missing Usage/Prerequisites section in http-client-csharp-mgmt README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58323 by `@JoshLove-msft`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58323 added `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts to both `http-client-csharp` and `http-client-csharp-mgmt` packages, and integrated doc regeneration into `Generate.ps1`. As part of this, the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` was auto-regenerated using `tspd doc`. However, the `tspd` tool only regenerates emitter option documentation — it does not preserve or generate the human-authored `## Usage` section.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is missing the `## Usage` section (with `### Prerequisite` and `### Customizing Generated Code` sub-sections) that exists in the parallel `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`\\n- The Prerequisites sub-section (Node.js and .NET SDK version requirements) is absent, leaving mgmt emitter users without setup guidance\\n- There is no link to a Customization Guide for mgmt-specific generated code (comparable to the one in the data-plane README)\\n- Neither README documents the new `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts — developers modifying emitter options manually need to know they can run `npm run regen-docs` to regenerate the README\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — Insert the following `## Usage` section after the `## Install` section and before the `## Emitter usage` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Usage\\n\\n### Prerequisite\\n\\n- Install [Node.js]((nodejs.org/redacted) 20 or above. (Verify by running `node --version`)\\n- Install [**.NET 10.0 SDK**]((dotnet.microsoft.com/redacted) for your specific platform. (or a higher version within the 10.0.\\\\* band)\\n\\n### Customizing Generated Code\\n\\nFor detailed instructions on how to customize the generated C# code for Azure management libraries, see the [Customization Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md).\\n\\n### Regenerating Documentation\\n\\nThe emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run:\\n\\n```bash\\nnpm run regen-docs\\n```\\n\\nThis rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter).\\n```\\n\\n#### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — Add a \\\"Regenerating Documentation\\\" sub-section under `## Usage`, after `### Customizing Generated Code`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Regenerating Documentation\\n\\nThe emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run:\\n\\n```bash\\nnpm run regen-docs\\n```\\n\\nThis rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter).\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNot applicable — these are tool/generator package READMEs, not Azure SDK client library packages. No snippet test files apply.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` should contain these sections after the fix:\\n1. `## Install` ✅ (already present)\\n2. `## Usage` ❌ **missing** — add it\\n   - `### Prerequisite`\\n   - `### Customizing Generated Code`\\n   - `### Regenerating Documentation`\\n3. `## Emitter usage` ✅ (already present)\\n4. `## Emitter options` ✅ (already present, auto-generated)\\n\\nReference: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order from the repository root:\\n\\n1. Verify the README changes look correct manually — no build is needed for pure documentation edits to these generator packages\\n2. Optionally, to confirm `regen-docs` still works after the edit (note: `tspd` only regenerates the `## Emitter options` section, so the `## Usage` section added manually will be preserved):\\n   ```powershell\\n   cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt\\n   npm install\\n   npm run regen-docs\\n   ```\\n   Check that the `## Usage` section is still present in the regenerated file (it should be, since `tspd` only affects the emitter options sections it generates).\\n\\n\\u003c/details\\u003e\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u003e [!TIP]\\n\\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign
